title: hoya lanceolata subsp. bella
intro: Hoya lanceolata subsp. bella, also known as the Bella Hoya or Sweetheart Hoya, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and fragrant, star-shaped flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hoya lanceolata subsp. bella include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of digestive disorders. In herbal preparations, the leaves of this plant are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
